question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 15?
narrative_context: Iran rejected US 15-point plan and publicly denies peace talks have occurred (Al Jazeera live blog, April 1 2026)
Israel struck IRGC navy commander and Isfahan targets; Iran fired missiles at central Israel (Al Jazeera, April 1 2026)
VP Vance chided Netanyahu for overselling regime change, limiting US diplomatic leverage (WaPo, April 1 2026)
Pakistan backchannel confirmed but Iran publicly refuses to negotiate; Trump paused energy strikes until April 6 (Al Jazeera, April 1 2026)
thesis: Market prices ceasefire at 28.5%; reality is ~9%. The military escalation is active (last 48 hours: major airstrikes and missile exchanges), Iran has explicitly rejected talks, and even a Pakistani backchannel is going nowhere. VP Vance's remarks about limited leverage suggest the US side understands the diplomatic impasse.
x_thread: Polymarket: US-Iran ceasefire by April 15? 28.5%. We see 9%. Here's the gap: the market prices in diplomatic breakthrough. Ground reality: escalation.
Iran rejected US 15-point plan. Publicly denies talks. Last 48 hours: Israel struck IRGC targets + Isfahan; Iran fired missiles at central Israel.
Even Pakistan's backchannel is stalled. VP Vance told Netanyahu he's overselling regime change—a coded admission the US has limited diplomatic leverage.
Markets love hope. But hope ≠ probability. Escalation trajectory continues. Ceasefire by April 15 unlikely. Full thesis: https://dub.sh/pb-x
telegram_post:
⚡ **US-Iran Ceasefire by April 15? Market: 28.5% | Reality: ~9%**

**The disconnect:** Market prices in diplomatic breakthrough. Reality is military escalation.

**Ground truth:**
• Iran rejected US 15-point plan and denies peace talks exist (as of April 1)
• Active escalation: Israel struck IRGC navy commander + Isfahan; Iran fired missiles at central Israel (last 48h)
• Pakistan backchannel stalled; Iran refuses to negotiate
• VP Vance admitted to Netanyahu that he's overselling regime change—admission of limited US diplomatic leverage

**Why markets are wrong:** 28% prices in a dramatic diplomatic reversal mid-active escalation. The trajectory is wrong. This is ~9% probability at best.
